symbols.*: Fine, very fine, and solid silver are mutually exclusive.
symbols.†: Meteoric, orichalcum, and solid silver are mutually exclusive – although meteoric and orichalcum weapons can be silver-coated.

weapons.name: Balanced
weapons.effects: +1 to skill with a melee or thrown weapon, or a projectile – or +1 Acc for a blowpipe, bow, or crossbow. Off limits for fist loads and sticks.

weapons.name: Bow-Harp
weapons.effects: A bow that can be used as either a weapon or a harp on any given turn. Used by bards. Adds 1.5 lbs. to bow weight.

weapons.name: Climber’s
weapons.effects: Blade, prong, and/or handle design lets you climb with the weapon ready. Gives +1 to Climbing skill (a pair still gives just +1).
weapons.allowed_on: Any hatchet, jutte, pick, sai, sickle, tonfa, or one-handed axe

weapons.name: Dwarven
weapons.effects: Changes a Parry of 0U to 0, letting a weapon that can’t normally parry and attack on the same turn do just that. Doesn’t prevent the weapon from becoming unready after attacking (‡ on ST).

weapons.name: Elven
weapons.effects: Lets a bow shoot at +2 to ST for range and damage purposes; e.g., a ST 11 elf could draw a ST 13 bow.

weapons.name: Fine
weapons.effects: -1 to odds of breakage (Parrying Heavy Weapons, Exploits, p. 49) and +1 to cutting and impaling damage for a melee or thrown weapon, or a projectile – or +20% to 1/2D and Max for a blowpipe, bow, or crossbow.
weapons.allowed_on: Projectiles, and crushing- or impaling-only melee or thrown weapons: +2 CF. Fencing weapons (p. 81), swords (pp. 81-82), blowpipes, bows, and crossbows: +3 CF. Other cutting melee or thrown weapons: +9 CF.

weapons.name: Meteoric
weapons.effects: Meteoric iron is immune to magic – Bladeturning, Missile Shield, Steel wraith, and so on won’t stop it, and Making and Breaking spells can’t destroy it. It can’t be magical, compose a power item, or benefit from weapon-enhancing spells or magical repairs.

weapons.name: Orichalcum
weapons.effects: Orichalcum weapons won’t break. Non-orichalcum weapons have +2 to odds of breakage when parrying heavy orichalcum ones.

weapons.name: Ornate
weapons.effects: Jewels, gold, etc. Improves reactions from buyers (and gullible hirelings!).
weapons.allowed_on: All weapons except projectiles and sticks
weapons.cost_factor: +1 to reactions for +1 CF, +2 for +4 CF, or +3 for +9 CF.

weapons.name: Silver
weapons.effects: Metal melee weapons, thrown weapons, or projectiles can be made of solid silver pure enough to exploit monster vulnerabilities, but have +2 to odds of breakage: +19 CF. Silver coating for such weapons doesn’t worsen breakage, but affects monsters less (¥2 injury becomes ¥1.5, ¥3 becomes ¥2, and ¥4 becomes ¥3): +2 CF.
weapons.allowed_on: Metal melee weapons, thrown weapons, or projectiles

weapons.name: Very Fine
weapons.effects: -2 to odds of breakage and +2 to cutting and impaling damage.

shields.name: Dwarven
shields.effects: Hefty iron instead of wood. Adds +1 damage with a shield bash or rush (cumulative with +1 for a spike) and makes the shield fireproof. Doubles weight!

shields.name: Fine
shields.effects: Shield has the same DB at only 3/4 of its usual weight.

shields.name: Meteoric
shields.effects: Resists magic as for the weapon modifier (p. 106), adds +1 damage with a shield bash or rush (cumulative with +1 for a spike), and is fireproof. Doubles weight!

shields.name: Mirrored
shields.effects: Polished or silvered to reflect gaze attacks. A successful Block against such an attack allows an immediate Shield roll to redirect the curse at anyone within its usual range. If the gaze allows a resistance roll, the target of a reflected gaze resists at +3.
shields.allowed_on: Dwarven, meteoric, or orichalcum shields: +2 CF. Other shields: +6 CF.

shields.name: Orichalcum
shields.effects: Shield has the same DB at only 2/3 of its usual weight, gives non-orichalcum weapons +2 to odds of breakage when parrying a shield bash or rush, and is fire proof.

shields.name: Ornate
shields.effects: Also allowed on cloaks. Impresses plebes as for the weapon modifier (p. 106): +1 to reactions for +1 CF, +2 for +4 CF, or +3 for +9 CF.

armor.name: Dragonhide
armor.effects: Heavy leather armor (DR 2) can be dragonhide. This provides from +1 to +4 DR, depending on the dragon, and double that DR bonus against fire. (Dragonhide with up to +7 DR – and unusual dragonhide that doubles its bonus vs. acid, cold, or other damage – exists but isn’t for sale.) It also gives -3 reactions from dragons! Weight and cost depend on DR bonus: +1 DR (+2 vs. fire) is 1.25¥ weight and +29 CF; +2 DR (+4 vs. fire) is 1.5¥ weight and +35 CF; +3 DR (+6 vs. fire) is 1.75¥ weight and +41 CF; and +4 DR (+8 vs. fire) is 2¥ weight and +47 CF.

armor.name: Dwarven
armor.effects: Dwarves can forge DR 6-9 plate armor (note [3]) to be harder! This gives +1 DR without raising weight.

armor.name: Elven
armor.effects: Elven mail uses the higher DR listed for the base armor against all damage – don’t reduce its DR vs. crushing attacks.

armor.name: Fine
armor.effects: Expertly fitted, with no waste material. Offers full DR at 3/4 of the usual weight. Only fits wearers whose height and weight match the original owner’s!

armor.name: Giant-Spider Silk
armor.effects: Improves light cloth armor (DR 1) to DR 2 without raising weight – and allows it to be worn under other armor (+2 DR) without -1 to DX for layering.

armor.name: Meteoric
armor.effects: Resists magic as for the weapon modifier (p. 106).

armor.name: Orichalcum
armor.effects: Gives full DR at just 1/3 of the usual weight!

armor.name: Ornate
armor.effects: Dress to impress, exactly as for the weapon modifier (p. 106).
armor.cost_factor: +1 to reactions for +1 CF, +2 for +4 CF, or +3 for +9 CF.

armor.name: Spiked
armor.effects: Every turn, roll DX-4 to stab each enemy in close combat with you as a free action; success deals 1d-2 impaling damage. In addition, anyone who strikes such armor with an unarmed attack takes this damage to the attacking body part – and if the attack is a bite, grapple, or anything under Slams, Tramples, and Overruns (Exploits, p. 40), the attacker suffers maximum damage (4 points).
armor.allowed_on: Any plate armor (note [3]) that covers the body or more

armor.name: Thieves’
armor.effects: Blackened mail woven for maximum flexibility, minimum noise. Find the total weight of your gear with and without this armor, and use weight without to figure encumbrance penalties (p. 12) to Acrobatics, Climbing, Stealth, and other skills (but not to Move or Dodge).
